sanskrit: दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि। दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास॥
transliteration: daṃṣṭrākarālāni ca te mukhāni dṛṣṭvaiva kālānala-sannibhāni / diśo na jāne na labhe ca śarma prasīda deveśa jagannivāsa //
literal_meaning: Having seen Your faces terrible with tusks, blazing like the fire of universal destruction, I know not the directions, nor do I find peace; be gracious, O Lord of gods, O refuge of the worlds.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confronts the overwhelming, annihilating aspect of the Absolute—his ego-boundaries dissolve, producing vertigo, disorientation, and a plea for mercy.
psychological_state: Acute dissociative overwhelm: loss of spatial orientation, inability to self-soothe, regression to a child-like plea for comfort.
trigger_scenario: A war-photographer suddenly zooms in on mangled bodies; the screen fills with death, the room spins, and the only words that come are 'Please… make it stop.'
anchor_text: When the infinite shows its face, surrender your maps and cry for mercy.
